altText.1: Question 1. In paragraph 3, what is mmoosstt lliikkeellyy the narrator’s point of view of Stephanie? 1 A Stephanie likes to talk with many people on the bus. B Sometimes Stephanie acts younger than she really is. C Stephanie likes to stay friends with people for a long time. D Sometimes Stephanie acts in a mean way toward a friend.
altText.2: Question 2. What does the phrase “left out” mean as used in paragraph 7? A watched B alone C troubled D careful
altText.3: Question 3. Which quotation best shows how Rosa and Stephanie usually act with each other? A “Rosa and Stephanie did everything together.” (paragraph 2) “She walked away to join Toni and Vanessa, and Rosa was left B : » standing alone.” (paragraph 5) Cc “Rosa tried not to check the mirror to see if Stephanie had noticed” (paragraph 7) D “But Rosa walked past her and slid into the empty seat next to Kiara.” (paragraph 8)
altText.4: Question 4. In paragraphs 16 through 20, the reader can see that Rosa changes from 4 A trying to avoid someone to making new friends B feeling sad about something to having a good time C wanting to upset her friend to wanting to include her D having a good time to feeling angry at someone
altText.5: Question 5. In paragraph 21, what does the phrase “Stephanie brightened” mean? 5 A She became smarter. B She moved into the sunlight. C She changed into dressy clothes. D She became happier.
altText.6: Question 6. Which sentence bbeesstt supports the central message of the story? 6 A Stephanie makes room for Rosa to sit next to her on the bus. B Stephanie tells Rosa that she is going to the pool with someone else. C Rosa tells Stephanie that she has plans with Kiara. D Rosa asks Stephanie if she wants to go to the pool with her and Kiara.
altText.7: Question 7. What question does paragraph 3 help to answer? 7 A How do ears take in sounds? B What do eardrums do? C How do noises travel? D What do sounds feel like?
altText.8: Question 8. Which sentence explains why there are no sounds in outer space? A __ Sound waves cannot travel fast enough to be heard in space. B Sound waves cannot bounce off things in space. C There is no air in space for sound waves to travel through. D There are no people or animals in space to hear sound waves.
altText.9: Question 9. What happens right after sound waves cause the eardrum to vibrate? A The brain makes sense of what is heard. B Nerve hairs send signals to the brain. C Sound waves travel through the ear canal. D Some sound waves cause lower sounds.
altText.10: Question 10. Which phrase from the passage best explains the meaning of “silence” as it is used in paragraph 7? A “There are no sounds” (paragraph 7) B “Because there is no air” (paragraph 7) C “sound waves bounce back” (paragraph 8) D “clap your hands in an empty room” (paragraph 8)
altText.11: Question 11. Which sentence best states the main idea of the passage? A Some noises are loud and others are quiet. B Sound waves do not travel through all things the same way. C Vibrations create sound waves that let us hear noises. D There is no air or sound in outer space.
altText.12: Question 12. Based on the passage, what does the author most likely think about how people hear? A Learning how people understand sounds is very interesting. B__Itis strange that all sounds do not return to where they start. C _Itis hard to find out how people make sense of sounds. D The fact that there is no sound in space is a problem.
